hero.question: Will annual inflation increase by ≥2.8% in March?
hero.prob_take: The market is pricing in a 94.2% chance of inflation hitting 3.8% or higher in March, which means traders have essentially called the outcome. The consensus looks lazy here: CPI prints are volatile month-to-month, base effects matter enormously, and betting 20-to-1 odds on any economic data point 2 months out ignores tail risk that shows up regularly.

daily_take.headline: Inflation at 94% Odds. The Market's Pricing in a Done Deal.
daily_take.deck: March inflation odds dropped 1 point overnight to 94.2%, but that's noise covering a real signal: the market has essentially priced in certainty. With a 2.8% threshold and most forecasters calling 2.9-3.1% CPI growth, bettors are treating this as a fait accompli. Sharp players should ask why. If core inflation cools faster than consensus expects, this crashes below 80%. That's your edge: fade the certainty.
